content: Just as the linguistic definition of reflexion encompasses both light reflection (a physical passive process) and deliberate cognitive reflection, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to split their workflow into two complementary stages: a passive 'reflection' stage that mirrors inconsistent prior outputs back unchanged like a reflected light beam, and an active 'reconsideration' stage that iteratively improves on those mirrored outputs. This dual structure leverages the inconsistent solving perspectives at the core of existing LLM self-reflection research to reduce unnecessary computation while amplifying contrast between flawed and improved responses.
